pr.title: >50% fuzzy match on all remaining item TUs
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large decomp/matching pass over the remaining low-fuzzy-match item translation units, adding substantial C implementations, item variable/attribute struct detail, header prototypes, and small call-site signature fixes across item and related fighter code. The author explicitly described the work as Claude-assisted but heavily human-cleaned, and reviewer feedback focused mainly on correcting include style for baselib and MSL math headers.
what_changed: Raised fuzzy-match coverage for many remaining item translation units, with large additions in itseakneedlethrown.c, itdosei.c, itmewtwoshadowball.c, itunknown.c, itpikachutjoltair.c, itkirbycutterbeam.c, ithitodeman.c, itzgshell.c, itgreatfoxlaser.c, itlipstickspore.c, itclinkmilk.c, itpatapata.c, itklap.c, itclimbersstring.c, and itkyasarin.c.

Expanded item headers with more specific ItemVars and attribute structs, including itCLinkMilk_ItemVars, itHitodemanAttributes, itKirby2F23_ItemVars, itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars, itSeakNeedleThrown_ItemVars fields, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itKyasarin_ItemVars/Attributes, itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars/DatAttrs, itUnknown_ItemVars/Attributes, and itGreatFoxLaser_Attrs/ItemVars refinements.
Changed Sheik needle thrown logic table entries in it_2725.c from it_803F6F50 to it_803F6F50.states.
Simplified it_80279C48 signature from taking Item_GObj* plus Article* to only Item_GObj*, deriving the article/item data internally; updated itchicorita.c call site accordingly.
Adjusted ftSeak shootNeedles so the return value of it_802AFEA8 is no longer assigned back to item_gobj.
Adjusted ftCo_800C74F4 comparison from it_802EAF28(...) == NULL to == 0U, implying the function is treated as an unsigned/scalar result rather than a pointer in that context.

Added fuller implementations or partial implementations for item behavior functions such as C-Link milk spawning/parent notification, climbers string spawning/accessory handling, and Dosei helper inlines for rotation/animation speed.

Replaced some generic or incorrectly borrowed item var union usage with item-specific names, for example clinkmilk and climbersstring instead of unrelated gamewatchchef, bombhei, or it_266F-style fields.
smart_moves: The pass paired broad TU matching work with header struct refinement, which makes future matching easier by replacing opaque pads and wrong union aliases with item-specific fields at known offsets.
The it_80279C48 cleanup removed an apparently unnecessary Article* parameter and made callers rely on Item-owned article data, reducing signature noise and improving type accuracy.
Using comments with item-private offsets such as ip+DD4, ip+DF0, and explicit field offsets preserved layout evidence while still allowing incremental naming.
The work avoided overclaiming final full matches: the PR goal was fuzzy improvement over a threshold, and the author acknowledged uncertainty and human cleanup after AI-generated drafts.
Changing logic table references to .states for it_803F6F50 made the table entry use the intended state table member rather than the surrounding object/struct.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs below a fuzzy-match threshold, a productive path is to first recover local item vars/attributes and call signatures, then clean up function bodies around those layouts.
AI-generated decomp drafts can be useful for volume but need careful human review for include style, type correctness, struct field ownership, and misleading borrowed union members.
When an item function can derive Article or attributes from Item state, prefer the narrower real signature and fix call sites rather than preserving extra guessed parameters.
Avoid assigning return values when the original code likely calls a function only for side effects; ftSk_SpecialN.c showed this with it_802AFEA8.
Scalar return comparisons should not be forced into pointer idioms; ft_0C31.c changed a NULL comparison to 0U for it_802EAF28.
Item private data often begins at xDD4/ip+DD4; preserving offset comments while adding fields helps later verification and union cleanup.
Large fuzzy-match PRs should expect review churn on mundane project conventions such as include path style.
naming_conventions: Item-private union structs use item-specific names like itCLinkMilk_ItemVars, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itKirbyCutterBeam_ItemVars, itKirby2F23_ItemVars, itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars, and itSeakNeedleThrown_ItemVars.
Attribute structs use item-specific names such as itHitodemanAttributes, itKyasarinAttributes, itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs, itGreatFoxLaser_Attrs, and itUnknownAttributes.
Unknown fields retained offset-based names like x0, x4, xDD4, xDF0, x1C8, and x6C, often with comments mapping both struct-relative and item-private offsets.
Inline helper names in itdosei.c used descriptive item-scoped prefixes such as itDosei_SetFacingAngle, itDosei_SetSpeed, itDosei_SetRotX, and itDosei_SetRotY.
Internal helper functions remained address-based where semantic names were not established, for example it_802C248C, fn_802C28DC, fn_802C2AF4, and it_802C33B8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced item-specific struct fields and corrected field types to shape codegen, for example changing itDosei_ItemVars.xDF0 from f32 to s32 and splitting arrays/pads into Vec3, f32, s16, or s32 fields.
Used small static inline wrappers around HSD_JObj operations in itdosei.c, likely to match repeated rotation/speed code patterns while keeping call sites concise.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in several newly implemented functions to preserve stack layout where needed.
Changed table initializers to reference a .states member, which can affect emitted data and match table layout.
Adjusted return-value use and comparison literals to better match expected codegen, such as dropping assignment from it_802AFEA8 and comparing to 0U.
Recovered spawn setup patterns with SpawnItem initialization, parent gobj assignment, facing_dir, zero velocity, Item_80268B18, GET_ITEM, and Item_8026AB54 attachment for item creation routines.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned leaving `it_804DC878` as a TODO needing `.sdata2`, asking whether there was a reason not to handle it immediately.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Same gobj include-style issue as prior comment.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Apply the same include-style correction for math.h noted in prior comments.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Fix include style as in previous math include comments.

follow_up_queries: Inspect final merged files for whether reviewer include-style requests were applied, especially `baseline/gobj.h` versus `baselib/gobj.h` and `<MSL/math.h>` versus `<math.h>`.
Check later PRs that continue matching the same item TUs after this fuzzy >50% pass.
Search for remaining uses of unrelated item var union fields in item-specific files, such as gamewatchchef, bombhei, or it_266F in nonmatching item contexts.
Verify whether `it_804DC878` placement in `.sdata2` was later resolved.
Compare `it_80279C48` callers after this PR to ensure all sites use the narrowed signature consistently.
